components.text:
I encountered the account of the Jade Artifact third-hand.

A wandering scholar — one of those rare men who had spent decades gathering fragments of history others had discarded — showed it to me during a chance meeting at a mountain waypost.

He had spent thirty years collecting transcriptions of it. Each one slightly different. Each one missing what the others had.

The Jade Artifact itself he had never seen.

He described it as a sealed jade object — no inscription on the surface, no recognizable formation. When held by an ordinary person, nothing occurred.

When a cultivator infused it with Qi above a certain threshold, a voice spoke from within.

Always the same voice. Always the same words. And then silence again.

He handed me his reconstruction. I have copied it here as faithfully as I was able.

—

'Damn it all. Why could those brats not have thought for a minute? Why else would that frozen turkey propose such a bet with everyone present? And why would they let that Traitor speak for them before I.'

'I, the Gr██t Imm█████ Phoenix — listened to the words of a pathetic newborn chick. A traitor, no less. A thousand years raised by our Clan and he betrays us like that. He is lucky those icy freaks took him with them, or I would have eviscerated his Heart Flame and used his blood to water my garden.'

'And what good is spreading our bloodlines to these lesser worlds? Those ants — what will they do with them? Merely seeing my higher self would corrupt their little forms. And that Block of Ice wants me to go about sharing my bloodline?

'Still. He never said anything about Purity or Grade.'

'Even in this decrepit mortal body lies a bloodline far more powerful than anything a mortal would ever have seen. A droplet diluted with some Red Mist should satisfy those Court enforcers. And I will leave a marker in the blood — so if someone talented is born with it, my ungrateful Clan can find them when they Ascend.

'I should finish this quickly.'

—

The transcription ended there. Or perhaps he had simply run out of pages.

Neither of us could say who the voice belonged to. The Gr██t Imm█████ Phoenix was a title, not a name. No record I have found since confirms such a being ever walked any world I know the name of.

But bloodlines exist. The Phoenix. The Draconic. The Tailed Fox. The Beast.

They are in this world. I have seen them.

And if the recording is genuine — if every bloodline was placed here deliberately, diluted and marked, as tokens of a wager between beings beyond our comprehension — then every carrier holds something left behind.

Not given. Not fated.

Left.
